title: Class inheritance

canonicalExplanation.summary: Class inheritance reuses and specializes behavior only when every subtype can honor the base contract, with superclass construction and protected/public surface controlled deliberately.

sections.heading: Start here
sections.body: Start with this case: Circle extends Shape because every Circle supports the complete Shape area contract and initializes required Shape state through super(...). Your goal is to evaluate and implement a small inheritance hierarchy without exposing mutable representation. Work through the example one step at a time, then use the boundary to check your answer.

sections.heading: Before you begin
sections.body: Review these declared prerequisites first: Constructors and invariants; Interfaces. If one is unfamiliar, revisit it before continuing.

sections.heading: Language-specific operational model
sections.body: Class inheritance reuses and specializes behavior only when every subtype can honor the base contract, with superclass construction and protected/public surface controlled deliberately. This model is applied through the declared Java: Beginner to Intermediate behavior rather than a language-neutral slogan.

sections.heading: Boundary and failure model
sections.body: Private representation is not inherited as accessible state, constructors chain, final restricts extension or override, and field hiding does not provide polymorphic state. The learner must reject this documented misconception: Inheritance is appropriate whenever two classes share fields or helper code, even if the subtype rejects base operations.

includes: Extend a base class only for a valid substitution relationship and initialize base state correctly.
Evaluate and implement a small inheritance hierarchy without exposing mutable representation.
excludes: Private representation is not inherited as accessible state, constructors chain, final restricts extension or override, and field hiding does not provide polymorphic state.

outcomes: Evaluate and implement a small inheritance hierarchy without exposing mutable representation.

examples.title: Class inheritance: direct language case
examples.situation: Circle extends Shape because every Circle supports the complete Shape area contract and initializes required Shape state through super(...).
walkthrough: Step 1 — Observe the concrete Java: Beginner to Intermediate state or tool result: Circle extends Shape because every Circle supports the complete Shape area contract and initializes required Shape state through super(...).
Step 2 — Use the core rule above to predict one observable result.
Step 3 — Verify the result with this correction: Use inheritance only for a valid substitution relationship; otherwise extract a collaborator or shared helper and compose it.
examples.result: The example meets the atomic outcome because Use inheritance only for a valid substitution relationship; otherwise extract a collaborator or shared helper and compose it.

examples.title: Class inheritance: boundary and transfer case
examples.situation: A read-only collection subtype that throws from an inherited mutator violates substitution, so the design uses a narrower interface or composition.
walkthrough: Step 1 — Name what changed from the direct case: A read-only collection subtype that throws from an inherited mutator violates substitution, so the design uses a narrower interface or composition.

Step 2 — Keep the language boundary explicit: Private representation is not inherited as accessible state, constructors chain, final restricts extension or override, and field hiding does not provide polymorphic state.
Step 3 — Reject the tempting but incorrect rule: Inheritance is appropriate whenever two classes share fields or helper code, even if the subtype rejects base operations.
examples.result: This case demonstrates the declared outcome: Evaluate and implement a small inheritance hierarchy without exposing mutable representation.

misconceptions.mistakenBelief: Inheritance is appropriate whenever two classes share fields or helper code, even if the subtype rejects base operations.
misconceptions.correction: Use inheritance only for a valid substitution relationship; otherwise extract a collaborator or shared helper and compose it.
misconceptions.diagnosticPrompt: Predict both examples and identify the first observable contradiction in this belief: Inheritance is appropriate whenever two classes share fields or helper code, even if the subtype rejects base operations.

analogy.example: a plug-and-socket system in which contracts describe compatible behavior while components hide their implementation can introduce Class inheritance, after the canonical Java: Beginner to Intermediate rule is stated first.
limitations: overload resolution, method hiding, constructor order, and multiple interface defaults require Java-specific rules

recap.summary: You should now be able to evaluate and implement a small inheritance hierarchy without exposing mutable representation. Keep this boundary in view: Private representation is not inherited as accessible state, constructors chain, final restricts extension or override, and field hiding does not provide polymorphic state.
